steps.title: Map the passage structure
steps.content: The passage has three parts: it introduces convergent evolution as a phenomenon, describes two studies of the phenomenon (Chan et al. on shared genetic mechanisms and Zwaan et al. on different genetic mechanisms), then mentions Green and Extavour's third study evaluating both types of convergence. The correct answer must match that exact sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — It details the development of a field of scientific inquiry, gives examples of three studies that were important to this development, and speculates on how future studies can develop these ideas.: Adds speculation about future studies, which is not in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — It explains a widespread assumption about a phenomenon, describes two studies that are based on that assumption, and explains why the assumption is unfounded.: Frames the passage as debunking an assumption; the passage does not do this. ✗
  • Choice C — It discusses a particular study of a phenomenon, elaborates on how two other studies have approached this phenomenon in the past, and critiques the first study's approach.: Introduces a critique that does not appear in the passage and reverses the focus. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes a phenomenon, provides examples of two studies of that phenomenon, and mentions a third study of that phenomenon.: Matches the passage exactly: phenomenon introduced, two study examples given, third study mentioned. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
